channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track has officially launched its Call for Applications for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026, offering catalytic funding and long-term support to innovators developing responsible AI solutions that improve the lives of persons with disabilities across Africa. Announced during the Global Data Festival […]

The post Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track has officially launched its Call for Applications for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026, offering catalytic funding and long-term support to innovators developing responsible AI solutions that improve the lives of persons with disabilities across Africa.

Announced during the Global Data Festival 2026, this initiative is designed to identify and support high-potential post-MVP ventures that are already building practical AI-driven solutions capable of creating measurable social impact across the continent.

Selected ventures will receive an intensive 18-month support package that combines funding, technical guidance, community-based user testing, and access to strategic partnerships to help scale inclusive technologies responsibly and sustainably.

Applications close on 17 July 2026.

About the HAIDI Innovation Track

The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track is focused on advancing disability inclusion through the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence.

Across Africa, millions of persons with disabilities continue to experience barriers when accessing education, employment, healthcare, caregiving support, digital services, and critical information systems. Many existing technologies are not designed with accessibility or inclusion in mind, limiting opportunities and participation.

Through this programme, HAIDI aims to support innovators who are creating AI-powered solutions that address these challenges while ensuring that disability communities are actively involved in the design, testing, and improvement of these technologies.

The programme prioritizes innovations that are not only technologically strong but also socially impactful, user-centered, and scalable across diverse African contexts.

What Selected Ventures Will Receive

The programme provides far more than just financial support. Selected applicants will gain access to a comprehensive ecosystem designed to help them refine and scale their innovations effectively.

1. Grants of Up to KES 5,000,000

Selected ventures will receive catalytic grants of up to KES 5 million (or USD equivalent) to support the development, testing, and scaling of their AI solutions.

The funding can help ventures:

  • Improve product development
  • Expand accessibility features
  • Strengthen technical infrastructure
  • Conduct pilot implementations
  • Scale operations across new communities
  • Enhance research and validation efforts

2. Hands-On Technical and Product Support

Participants will receive direct technical guidance and product support throughout the 18-month programme.

This includes:

  • Product refinement support
  • Technical mentorship
  • AI development guidance
  • Responsible AI implementation support
  • Scaling and operational advice
  • Product usability improvement strategies

The programme aims to help ventures strengthen both the technical quality and real-world effectiveness of their solutions.

3. User Testing With Disability Communities Across Africa

One of the most important elements of the programme is direct engagement with disability communities.

Selected innovators will have opportunities to:

  • Test their products with real users
  • Gather feedback from persons with disabilities
  • Improve accessibility and usability
  • Validate impact in real-world settings
  • Ensure products are culturally and contextually relevant

This user-centered approach helps ensure that AI solutions are developed responsibly and inclusively rather than in isolation.

4. Access to Partnerships and Future Funding Opportunities

Participants will also gain exposure to a broader ecosystem of partners, experts, and funding networks.

Benefits include:

  • Strategic partnerships
  • Networking opportunities
  • Visibility within the AI and innovation ecosystem
  • Connections with funders and investors
  • Opportunities for future collaborations
  • Access to expert communities and advisors

This support can help ventures continue growing beyond the duration of the programme.

Focus Areas for Innovation

The HAIDI Innovation Track is looking for AI-driven solutions that address challenges affecting persons with disabilities across Africa.

Key areas of interest include:

  • Education
  • Employment
  • Mental health
  • Caregiving
  • Assistive technology
  • Accessibility tools
  • Inclusive communication systems
  • Disability-focused digital services
  • Community empowerment solutions

Innovations from related sectors that contribute to disability inclusion are also encouraged to apply.

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from a wide range of innovators and organizations working on inclusive AI technologies.

Eligible applicants include:

  • AI startups and SMEs
  • Individual innovators
  • Researchers
  • Research institutions
  • Universities
  • Multi-stakeholder consortia
  • Post-MVP ventures with existing AI solutions

Applicants should already have a functional solution or product that demonstrates strong potential for scaling and impact.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Artificial intelligence has the potential to significantly improve accessibility, independence, and inclusion for persons with disabilities. However, many AI systems globally are still developed without sufficient representation or accessibility considerations.

The HAIDI Innovation Track aims to change that by supporting African innovators who are building inclusive technologies with disability communities at the center of the process.

This initiative represents a major opportunity for innovators who want to:

  • Build socially impactful AI solutions
  • Scale accessibility-focused technologies
  • Collaborate with disability communities
  • Gain funding and technical support
  • Expand across African markets
  • Strengthen responsible AI practices

By investing in inclusive innovation, the programme seeks to help create a more accessible and equitable digital future across Africa.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is:

17 July 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications before the deadline.

About the Organizations Behind the Initiative

The AI for Development (AI4D) initiative supports responsible AI research and innovation across Africa.

The initiative is funded by:

  • FCDO
  • IDRC
  • Sida
  • Community Jameel

The programme is supported by the Global Center on AI Governance (GCG).

Final Thoughts

The Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is one of the most important opportunities currently available for innovators working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, accessibility, and social impact in Africa.

With substantial grant funding, long-term technical support, community-based testing, and strategic partnerships, the programme provides a powerful platform for scaling inclusive AI solutions that can improve the lives of persons with disabilities across the continent.

Innovators building impactful and responsible AI technologies are strongly encouraged to apply before the 17 July 2026 deadline.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Climate Litigation School 2026 is now accepting applications for its upcoming fully funded training programme taking place from 6–10 July 2026 at the Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa. This transformative initiative is jointly hosted by Natural Justice, the Centre for Environmental Rights, and the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Environmental Justice […]

The post Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Climate Litigation School 2026 is now accepting applications for its upcoming fully funded training programme taking place from 6–10 July 2026 at the Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa. This transformative initiative is jointly hosted by Natural Justice, the Centre for Environmental Rights, and the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Environmental Justice in Africa.

Designed to strengthen the capacity of emerging and established climate justice actors, the School offers an intensive, interdisciplinary learning experience that blends legal strategy, scientific evidence, community advocacy, and rights‑based approaches to climate litigation.

Overview of the Climate Litigation School 2026

The Climate Litigation School is a dedicated training space that brings together students, researchers, lawyers, and climate justice practitioners to deepen their understanding of how law can be used to confront the climate crisis. The programme emphasizes collaboration across disciplines, integrating legal expertise, environmental science, Indigenous knowledge, and community‑driven advocacy.

The 2026 edition of the School will:

  • Equip participants with practical skills in climate litigation.
  • Strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration between lawyers, scientists, activists, and community leaders.
  • Build a network of climate justice advocates across South Africa and the African continent.
  • Promote rights‑based, community‑centered approaches to environmental justice.

The programme is fully funded, covering wheelchair‑accessible accommodation, meals, and flights for selected participants.

Programme Dates and Location

  • Curriculum Dates: 6 – 10 July 2026
  • Venue: Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa
  • Funding: Fully funded (accommodation, meals, and flights covered)
  • Application Deadline: 16 June 2026

About the Climate Litigation School

The School is designed to build long‑term capacity among individuals working to advance climate justice. It goes beyond traditional legal training by focusing on real‑world, context‑specific challenges and opportunities in climate litigation.

Key Focus Areas

  1. Climate Litigation Strategies Participants will explore landmark climate cases, strategic litigation approaches, and emerging legal tools used to hold governments and corporations accountable.
  2. Environmental Governance The curriculum examines how governance systems shape environmental decision‑making and how legal interventions can strengthen accountability.
  3. Climate Science Integration The School emphasizes the importance of climate attribution science, environmental data, and impact projections in building strong legal cases.
  4. Community‑Lawyer Alliances A core component is the integration of community voices, ensuring that frontline communities are co‑creators of legal strategies.
  5. Indigenous and Traditional Knowledge The programme highlights the role of Indigenous knowledge systems in shaping culturally grounded, rights‑based litigation.

What Makes This School Unique

The Climate Litigation School stands out for its holistic, interdisciplinary approach. It brings together:

  • Legal practitioners
  • Environmental scientists
  • Indigenous knowledge holders
  • Community leaders
  • Climate justice activists
  • Policy researchers

This collaborative model ensures that litigation strategies are not only technically sound but also socially just, culturally relevant, and grounded in lived realities.

Participants will engage with leading experts working at the forefront of climate litigation, gaining insights into both national and continental climate justice movements.

Who Should Apply

Applications are welcomed from individuals who:

  • Are based in South Africa
  • Are working or studying in fields such as:
    • Law
    • Environmental science
    • Public policy
    • Climate justice activism
  • Are committed to applying the knowledge gained to support climate justice in their communities or professional work

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Demonstrated interest or experience in climate, environmental, or human rights work
  • Potential to contribute to and benefit from the programme
  • Commitment to advancing climate justice
  • Consideration of diversity, including racial and gender representation

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application portal. Deadline: 16 June 2026

Themes and Programme Areas

  • Legal Empowerment
  • Litigation
  • Climate Change
  • Environmental Justice
  • Defending Rights

Related Topics for Further Exploration

  • High Court seismic survey challenge
  • Africa’s energy transition delays
  • Deep-water drilling court hearings
  • Community power and climate justice

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more  fellowship opportunities here 

The post Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Heinrich Böll Foundation offers prestigious scholarship opportunities for highly motivated international students and doctoral candidates who wish to pursue higher education in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic excellence, social engagement, democratic values, and environmental sustainability. The programme primarily targets talented students and researchers from DAC (Development Assistance Committee) countries who aim […]

The post Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heinrich Böll Foundation offers prestigious scholarship opportunities for highly motivated international students and doctoral candidates who wish to pursue higher education in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic excellence, social engagement, democratic values, and environmental sustainability.

The programme primarily targets talented students and researchers from DAC (Development Assistance Committee) countries who aim to pursue a Master’s degree or PhD in Germany. The foundation supports individuals who demonstrate strong academic performance, political awareness, and commitment to social justice, human rights, and environmental responsibility.

This scholarship initiative is funded by the Federal Ministry of Education and Research (BMBF) and the Federal Foreign Office (AA), making it one of the most reputable fully supported academic funding opportunities in Germany.

About the Heinrich Böll Foundation

The Heinrich Böll Foundation is a German political foundation affiliated with the Green political movement. It is committed to:

  • Promoting democracy and human rights globally
  • Supporting environmental sustainability and climate justice
  • Encouraging gender equality and social inclusion
  • Strengthening civic education and political participation
  • Supporting international academic exchange and research development

Through its scholarship programme, the foundation builds a global network of young leaders who contribute to progressive social change.

Scholarship Overview: Tailwind for Talents Programme 2026–2027

The “Tailwind for Talents” scholarship supports international students and PhD researchers who plan to study in Germany. The programme provides financial assistance, academic support, and access to a strong global alumni network.

Key Details

  • Scholarship Provider: Heinrich Böll Foundation
  • Country: Germany
  • Study Level: Master’s and PhD
  • Application Periods:
    • Fall 2026: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026
    • Spring 2027: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027
  • Funding Type: Fully funded scholarship support (financial assistance and academic support)

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship is open to two main categories of applicants:

1. Master’s Students

Applicants must:

  • Have completed their first university degree outside Germany
  • Be planning to pursue a Master’s degree in Germany
  • Not have started living in Germany at the time of application
  • Come from DAC countries (priority group)

2. PhD Candidates

Applicants must:

  • Have completed a graduate degree abroad
  • Be applying for or planning to pursue a PhD programme in Germany
  • Not be residing in Germany at the time of application
  • Be international applicants from DAC countries (priority priority group)

Priority Regions: DAC Countries

Priority is given to applicants from DAC-listed developing countries. These typically include regions in:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Parts of the Middle East and Pacific regions

This ensures the scholarship supports students from underrepresented and developing regions.

Fields of Study

While open to many academic disciplines, strong preference is often given to candidates studying:

  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Environmental Studies
  • Law and Human Rights
  • Social Sciences
  • Economics and Development Studies
  • Climate Policy and Sustainability-related fields

Scholarship Benefits

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship offers more than financial support. It provides a complete academic and professional development ecosystem.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Financial support for living and academic expenses
  • Access to a strong academic and professional network in Germany
  • Participation in seminars, workshops, and training programmes
  • Integration into a global alumni community
  • Career development and leadership training opportunities
  • Exposure to German political, environmental, and social systems

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete and submit official application forms depending on their study level:

  • Application for Master’s students (PDF form)
  • Application for PhD candidates (PDF form)

Additional Requirements:

Applicants are typically expected to provide:

  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Motivation letter
  • Research proposal (for PhD applicants)
  • Proof of language proficiency (depending on programme requirements)
  • Curriculum vitae (CV)

Selection Criteria

Candidates are assessed based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Social and political engagement
  • Motivation and clarity of study/research goals
  • Commitment to democracy, sustainability, and human rights
  • Leadership potential and civic responsibility

Why This Scholarship Matters

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship is not just financial aid—it is a leadership development platform. It enables scholars to:

  • Study in one of Europe’s strongest academic systems
  • Join a global network of change-makers
  • Engage in environmental and social policy discussions
  • Build long-term careers in academia, governance, NGOs, and international organizations

Application Deadlines

  • Fall 2026 intake: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026
  • Spring 2027 intake: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027

Late applications are not accepted.

Learn more and apply here

Conclusion

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship 2026–2027 is an excellent opportunity for ambitious international students and researchers who want to study in Germany while contributing to global social, environmental, and political change. It is highly competitive and ideal for individuals committed to academic excellence and progressive leadership.

For more information, visit the official website. 

Discover more fully funded global scholarships on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding, a prestigious leadership development programme offered by The Asia Foundation (TAF) for emerging peacebuilders from Asia and the Pacific. Designed to support the next generation of leaders working in peacebuilding, conflict transformation, mediation, advocacy, policy, and community engagement, the fellowship provides a […]

The post William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding, a prestigious leadership development programme offered by The Asia Foundation (TAF) for emerging peacebuilders from Asia and the Pacific. Designed to support the next generation of leaders working in peacebuilding, conflict transformation, mediation, advocacy, policy, and community engagement, the fellowship provides a year-long professional development experience that combines international exposure, leadership training, networking, and project support.

Each year, the fellowship selects a small cohort of exceptional professionals who have demonstrated a commitment to advancing peace and addressing conflict challenges in their communities and countries. Through immersive learning experiences, engagement with global experts, and dedicated professional development funding, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge, networks, and skills needed to strengthen their impact.

For 2026, three fellows will be selected to participate in this highly competitive programme, which includes a two-week study tour in the United States, virtual leadership development seminars, and a professional development grant worth USD 2,500.

About the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding

The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is The Asia Foundation’s flagship leadership development initiative focused on peacebuilding and conflict transformation.

Established in 2004 by The Asia Foundation’s Board of Trustees, the fellowship honors William P. Fuller, who served as President of The Asia Foundation for 15 years. The programme reflects his commitment to nurturing emerging leaders dedicated to peace, stability, and social cohesion throughout Asia and the Pacific.

The fellowship is supported through privately endowed funds and seeks to empower professionals whose work contributes to building peaceful, inclusive, and resilient societies.

As conflict dynamics continue to evolve throughout the region, there is increasing demand for leaders capable of:

  • Bridging social, political, and cultural divides
  • Facilitating dialogue among diverse stakeholders
  • Promoting peaceful conflict resolution
  • Designing locally grounded peacebuilding solutions
  • Strengthening social cohesion
  • Supporting reconciliation processes
  • Addressing root causes of conflict

The Fuller Fellowship aims to strengthen the leadership capacity of individuals already working in these areas.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The Asia-Pacific region continues to experience complex conflict and peacebuilding challenges, including:

  • Political instability
  • Social fragmentation
  • Identity-based conflicts
  • Community-level disputes
  • Governance challenges
  • Resource conflicts
  • Emerging security concerns

Addressing these issues requires innovative leaders who can combine research, policy engagement, community-based approaches, and collaborative problem-solving.

The William P. Fuller Fellowship supports such leaders by helping them expand their influence, strengthen their networks, and gain access to global best practices in peacebuilding.

What the Fellowship Offers

The fellowship provides a comprehensive leadership development experience lasting one year.

1. Two-Week Study Tour in the United States

One of the most valuable components of the fellowship is an intensive study tour and professional exchange programme.

Selected fellows will travel to:

  • San Francisco Bay Area
  • New York City
  • Washington, D.C.

Study Tour Dates

10–21 November 2026

During the visit, fellows will engage with:

  • Peacebuilding practitioners
  • Policymakers
  • Academic experts
  • Civil society leaders
  • Researchers
  • Government representatives
  • International development professionals

The study tour provides exposure to diverse approaches to peacebuilding and conflict transformation from both local and global perspectives.

2. Leadership Development Seminars

Fellows will participate in virtual learning sessions delivered through The Asia Foundation’s Leadership & Exchange Programmes.

Topics may include:

  • Leadership development
  • Peacebuilding strategies
  • Conflict transformation
  • Negotiation and mediation
  • Policy engagement
  • Systems thinking
  • Strategic communication
  • Stakeholder engagement

3. Professional Development Grant

Each fellow receives:

USD 2,500 Professional Development Grant

The grant can be used to support:

  • A self-designed project
  • Professional learning goals
  • Research initiatives
  • Leadership development activities
  • Community engagement projects
  • Capacity-building efforts

This funding enables fellows to pursue initiatives that strengthen their long-term impact.

4. International Professional Network

Participants gain access to an influential network of:

  • Peacebuilding experts
  • Regional leaders
  • Civil society practitioners
  • Researchers
  • Policymakers
  • Alumni fellows

This network often becomes a long-term source of collaboration and professional support.

Fellowship Objectives

The programme seeks to:

  1. Strengthen leadership skills among emerging peacebuilders.
  2. Expand professional networks across Asia and the Pacific.
  3. Support innovative approaches to conflict transformation.
  4. Encourage cross-border collaboration.
  5. Promote dialogue and mutual understanding.
  6. Advance sustainable peacebuilding efforts throughout the region.
  7. Develop future leaders capable of responding to evolving conflict challenges.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship targets early- and mid-career professionals actively contributing to peacebuilding and conflict transformation.

Applicants may be working in areas such as:

  • Research
  • Public policy
  • Mediation
  • Community engagement
  • Peace advocacy
  • Dialogue facilitation
  • Human rights
  • Conflict resolution
  • Social cohesion
  • Governance
  • Development programming

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • 40 years old or younger during the programme year
  • Born after 13 October 1986

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must come from a country where The Asia Foundation has a presence.

U.S. Travel Eligibility

Applicants must be able to secure a visa to travel to the United States.

Special note:

Candidates from:

  • Myanmar
  • Afghanistan
  • Laos

must currently be based in the United States.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be fluent in:

  • Written English
  • Spoken English

Professional Experience

Strong candidates typically have:

  • At least three years of professional experience
  • Demonstrated leadership capacity
  • Experience advancing peacebuilding efforts through research or practice

Commitment Requirement

Applicants must be able to commit fully to all fellowship activities and programme dates.

Characteristics of Strong Candidates

The Asia Foundation is particularly interested in candidates who:

  • Demonstrate leadership potential
  • Are committed to peacebuilding in the Asia-Pacific region
  • Value collaboration and learning
  • Seek to expand professional networks
  • Show initiative and innovation
  • Are willing to complete self-directed learning
  • Have a clear vision for their future contributions to peacebuilding

Programme Impact

Since its creation, the fellowship has helped develop a growing network of peacebuilding leaders throughout Asia and the Pacific.

Participants often go on to:

  • Lead peace initiatives
  • Influence policy reforms
  • Conduct impactful research
  • Strengthen civil society organizations
  • Facilitate community dialogue
  • Build regional partnerships

The fellowship serves as a platform for long-term leadership development and collaboration.

Application Timeline

Applications Open

10 June 2026

Applications Close

12 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and prepare supporting materials well before the deadline.

APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply

The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is one of the most prestigious opportunities available for emerging peacebuilders in Asia and the Pacific.

The fellowship offers far more than professional training. It provides:

  • International exposure
  • Leadership development
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Access to leading experts
  • Financial support for professional growth
  • Long-term membership in a regional peacebuilding network

For professionals dedicated to promoting peace, dialogue, and conflict transformation, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to strengthen their leadership journey and amplify their impact.

Click HERE for more information about the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026.

Conclusion

The 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding represents an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders working to build more peaceful and inclusive societies across Asia and the Pacific. Through international exchanges, leadership development, mentorship, networking opportunities, and financial support, the fellowship equips participants with the tools needed to address complex peace and conflict challenges in their communities. As societies across the region navigate increasingly complex social and political dynamics, the need for innovative and collaborative peacebuilders has never been greater. This fellowship offers a powerful platform for rising leaders to expand their influence, strengthen their expertise, and contribute meaningfully to lasting peace.

Discover more global Fellowship and Asian opportunities on OFY.

The post William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career professionals and recent graduates through its Graduate Professional Programme (GPP) 2026. This position focuses on Security and Technology, specifically supporting Digital Tools such as the Cyber Policy Portal and Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal. Based in Geneva, Switzerland, with remote participation […]

The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career professionals and recent graduates through its Graduate Professional Programme (GPP) 2026. This position focuses on Security and Technology, specifically supporting Digital Tools such as the Cyber Policy Portal and Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal.

Based in Geneva, Switzerland, with remote participation options available, this 6-month role provides a structured entry point into international peace and security work within the United Nations system. The programme is designed for individuals passionate about emerging technologies, digital governance, cybersecurity policy, and global disarmament issues.

The Graduate Professional will work under the UNIDIR Security and Technology Programme, contributing directly to research support systems, communication tools, and policy knowledge platforms used by international stakeholders.

About UNIDIR and Its Mission

The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is an autonomous UN institute funded voluntarily and based in Geneva. It plays a critical role in:

  • Producing independent research on disarmament and global security
  • Facilitating international dialogue on emerging security threats
  • Supporting policy development on technological risks in security environments
  • Promoting innovative solutions to global peace and stability challenges

The Security and Technology Programme focuses on understanding how technological advancements impact international security, including:

  • Artificial intelligence governance
  • Cybersecurity policy frameworks
  • Digital transformation in security systems
  • Emerging technologies and global risk mitigation

Position Overview: Graduate Professional (Security and Technology – Digital Tools)

This role is designed to support the development, maintenance, and improvement of UNIDIR’s digital knowledge platforms, including:

  • Cyber Policy Portal
  • Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal

These platforms serve as key global resources for policymakers, researchers, and institutions working in international security and technology governance.

Key Position Details

  • Position Title: Graduate Professional (Security and Technology – Digital Tools)
  • Organization: United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR)
  • Location: Geneva, Switzerland or Remote
  • Duration: 6 months (with possible extension)
  • Employment Type: Full-time (100% occupational rate)
  • Start Date: Negotiable, as soon as possible
  • Closing Date: 15 June 2026
  • Stipend:
    • Remote: USD 750 per month
    • In-person Geneva: USD 1500 per month

Core Responsibilities

The selected Graduate Professional will support both technical and policy-related functions. Responsibilities include:

Digital Tools and Platform Support

  • Assist in maintaining and updating UNIDIR’s digital policy portals
  • Support troubleshooting and rapid resolution of technical issues
  • Contribute to improving platform usability and functionality

Research and Communication Support

  • Draft official communications, reports, and documentation
  • Assist in preparing policy briefs and analytical summaries
  • Support synthesis of research into accessible formats

Content Development and Outreach

  • Create infographics and communication materials
  • Support promotional activities for digital tools
  • Contribute to internal and external communication strategies

Coordination and Administrative Support

  • Participate in meetings, workshops, and conferences when required
  • Provide written and verbal briefings to teams
  • Support day-to-day coordination and administrative tasks

Required Skills and Competencies

This role requires a combination of technical literacy, analytical thinking, and communication skills.

Core Skills

  • Strong research and analytical abilities
  • Excellent writing and editing skills
  • Ability to summarize complex information into clear outputs
  • Project management and organizational capability
  • Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

Desired Interests

Candidates should demonstrate interest in:

  • International peace and security
  • Disarmament policy
  • Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies
  • United Nations system and global governance

UN Core Competencies

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Professionalism: High standards of integrity and responsibility
  • Communication: Clear and audience-adapted messaging
  • Teamwork: Collaboration across diverse cultural environments
  • Planning and Organization: Efficient time and task management
  • Client Orientation: Responsiveness to stakeholder needs

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this Graduate Professional Programme, applicants must meet one of the following:

Academic Requirements

  • Currently enrolled in a graduate programme (second university degree or higher), OR
  • Have completed a graduate degree within the last two years

Preference is given to candidates with backgrounds in:

  • International Relations
  • Political Science
  • International Law
  • International Security
  • Technology Policy or related fields

Experience Requirements

  • No strict professional experience required
  • Prior research experience is considered an advantage
  • Interest in AI, cybersecurity, or emerging technologies is highly desirable
  • Strong digital literacy is required

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (written and spoken) is mandatory
  • Knowledge of another UN language is an advantage

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package including:

  • Cover letter
  • Inspira Personal History Profile (PHP)
  • Short responses to screening questions (maximum 30 words each)

Application Format Instructions

  • All documents must be combined into a single PDF file
  • File name format: Sectec_SURNAME_Name_PHP-CL
  • Email submission required to: unidir.recruitment@un.org
  • Subject line: UNIDIR Security and Technology June 2026 GPP Application

Important Notes

  • CVs are not accepted in place of a PHP
  • Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered
  • Women are strongly encouraged to apply
  • Applicants must indicate preference for remote or in-person participation
  • Medical insurance coverage is required for participation

Screening Questions Overview

Applicants must address the following:

  • Eligibility based on current or recent graduate status
  • English fluency and communication ability
  • Research experience and analytical capability
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools and related software
  • Ability to manage and coordinate multiple tasks effectively

Deadline

The final deadline for application submission is 15 June 2026 (midnight Geneva time). Late submissions will not be accepted.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This Graduate Professional role offers a unique entry point into the United Nations system, particularly in one of the fastest-growing policy areas: the intersection of security and emerging technology.

Participants will gain:

  • Direct exposure to UN policy development processes
  • Experience working on global digital security tools
  • Practical skills in research, communication, and digital platforms
  • Professional experience within an international organization
  • Networking opportunities in global security and technology governance

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY,VISIT HERE

The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand (Fully Funded Fellowship Available)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026, a prestigious training programme organized by the Codification Division of the United Nations Office of Legal Affairs (OLA) in cooperation with the Government of the Kingdom of Thailand and the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and […]

The post United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand (Fully Funded Fellowship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026, a prestigious training programme organized by the Codification Division of the United Nations Office of Legal Affairs (OLA) in cooperation with the Government of the Kingdom of Thailand and the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP).

This highly respected programme offers legal professionals from across the Asia-Pacific region a unique opportunity to deepen their knowledge of international law, engage with leading experts and practitioners, and strengthen their capacity to contribute to international legal processes and institutions. The course will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, from 23 November to 16 December 2026 and will be conducted entirely in English.

For professionals working in government institutions, academia, international organizations, non-governmental organizations, and legal practice, the United Nations Regional Course in International Law represents one of the most valuable capacity-building opportunities in the field of international law.

The programme accommodates up to 26 participants and includes a limited number of fully funded fellowships as well as spaces for self-funded participants.

About the United Nations Regional Course in International Law

The United Nations Regional Course in International Law is part of the broader United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Appreciation of International Law.

The programme was established to strengthen understanding of international law among legal professionals and government officials while promoting the peaceful settlement of disputes and international cooperation.

Through intensive lectures, discussions, and practical learning opportunities, participants gain advanced knowledge of international legal principles and contemporary issues affecting the international community.

The Regional Course serves as an important platform for legal professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to exchange experiences, build networks, and enhance their expertise in international law.

Host Institutions

The 2026 Regional Course is organized through a collaboration between:

  • United Nations Office of Legal Affairs (OLA)
  • Codification Division of the United Nations
  • Government of the Kingdom of Thailand
  • United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP)

The course will be hosted at ESCAP facilities in Bangkok, Thailand.

Course Dates and Location

Location

Bangkok, Thailand

Venue

United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP)

Programme Dates

23 November – 16 December 2026

Language of Instruction

English

Number of Participants

Up to 26 participants

The programme will include:

  • Fellowship recipients
  • Self-funded participants

Objectives of the Programme

The United Nations Regional Course in International Law aims to:

  • Strengthen participants’ knowledge of international law.
  • Enhance understanding of contemporary legal issues.
  • Promote the teaching and dissemination of international law.
  • Build professional capacity among legal practitioners and policymakers.
  • Encourage international cooperation through legal frameworks.
  • Foster regional and international professional networks.

The course is specifically designed for professionals who are already working in fields related to international law and wish to expand their expertise.

What Participants Will Learn

Participants will receive intensive training covering key areas of international law and related legal disciplines.

The programme typically addresses:

Public International Law

  • Sources of international law
  • State responsibility
  • International legal obligations
  • Treaty law

International Organizations

  • United Nations system
  • Functions of international institutions
  • Global governance mechanisms

International Dispute Settlement

  • Peaceful settlement of disputes
  • International courts and tribunals
  • Arbitration mechanisms

Human Rights Law

  • International human rights frameworks
  • Treaty bodies
  • Monitoring mechanisms

International Humanitarian Law

  • Laws of armed conflict
  • Protection of civilians
  • Humanitarian obligations

International Economic Law

  • Trade law
  • Investment law
  • Economic cooperation

Emerging Legal Challenges

  • Climate change law
  • Cyber law
  • International security
  • Sustainable development

Participants benefit from interactions with distinguished academics, judges, diplomats, legal practitioners, and United Nations experts.

Fellowship Benefits

A limited number of selected participants will receive United Nations fellowships.

The fellowship covers:

International Travel

  • Economy-class airfare to and from Bangkok.

Accommodation

  • Accommodation provided for the duration of the programme.

Medical Insurance

  • Comprehensive medical insurance coverage during the course period.

Training Materials

  • All official course materials.

Living Stipend

Fellowship recipients receive a modest stipend designed to cover:

  • Food
  • Local transportation
  • Daily living expenses

The stipend follows United Nations fellowship regulations and reflects local living costs.

Accessibility Support

Reasonable accommodation may be provided upon request for participants with disabilities.

Self-Funded Participation

The Regional Course also accepts a limited number of self-funded participants.

Self-funded participants enjoy:

  • Access to all lectures and activities.
  • Participation in the same programme.
  • Equal academic standing.
  • The same certificate upon successful completion.

However, self-funded participants are responsible for:

  • International travel.
  • Accommodation.
  • Medical insurance.
  • Living expenses.

Training materials are provided to all participants regardless of funding status.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered, applicants must meet the following requirements.

Legal Background

Applicants must possess:

  • A legal education background.

Professional Experience

Candidates must have:

  • Professional experience in the field of international law.

The programme is intended for professionals rather than students.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Fluent in English.

As the entire programme will be conducted in English, participants should be able to actively engage in lectures and discussions.

Availability

Selected participants must commit to attending the entire course.

Partial attendance is not permitted.

Who Can Apply?

The programme is open to candidates from:

  • United Nations Member States in the Asia-Pacific region.
  • The observer State to the United Nations in the Asia-Pacific region.

Eligible applicants may come from:

Government Institutions

  • Ministries
  • Foreign affairs departments
  • Legal departments
  • Judicial institutions

Academic Institutions

  • Universities
  • Law faculties
  • Research institutes

International Organizations

  • Intergovernmental organizations
  • Regional organizations

Civil Society Organizations

  • NGOs working on legal and policy issues

Legal Professionals

  • Lawyers
  • Legal advisers
  • Policy experts

Current United Nations staff members are not eligible to apply.

Selection Process

Applications are reviewed through a competitive selection process.

The United Nations considers:

Professional Qualifications

Relevant legal education and expertise.

Scope of Professional Responsibilities

The extent to which international law forms part of the applicant’s work.

Relevance of Training

How participation will contribute to the applicant’s professional responsibilities.

Gender Balance

The programme promotes gender equality, and female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply.

Important FAQs

Can I Apply If I Am a Student?

No. The Regional Course is specifically intended for professionals with practical experience in international law.

Can I Reapply If I Was Previously Unsuccessful?

Yes. Applicants who were not selected in previous years may submit a new application.

Can I Attend Only Part of the Course?

No. Selected participants must attend the programme in its entirety.

Can Several Candidates Apply From the Same Country?

Yes. Countries are encouraged to nominate multiple qualified candidates.

Does the United Nations Charge Any Application Fee?

No. The United Nations does not charge any fees at any stage of the application process.

Applicants should never provide banking information during the application process.

Click HERE for more FAQ’s

Why You Should Apply

This programme provides a rare opportunity to:

  • Receive advanced training from UN experts.
  • Expand your international law expertise.
  • Build professional networks across the Asia-Pacific region.
  • Strengthen your career in diplomacy, international organizations, government service, academia, or legal practice.
  • Experience a prestigious United Nations educational programme.
  • Gain a deeper understanding of contemporary international legal challenges.

For professionals seeking to advance their careers in international law, this course offers exceptional value and recognition.

Application Deadline

Applications for the 2026 Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific are open until:

29 June 2026

Late submissions may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the Official Application Form and submit all required documentation to the Codification Division of the United Nations Office of Legal Affairs.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review:

  • Programme brochure
  • Application form
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Frequently Asked Questions

before submitting their applications.

Contact Information

For additional inquiries, applicants may contact:

Codification Division, United Nations Office of Legal Affairs

Email: rcil-asia@un.org

Visit HERE to read more about the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand.

Conclusion

The United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 is one of the most prestigious professional training opportunities available for legal professionals in the region. Through intensive instruction, engagement with leading experts, and access to a global network of practitioners, participants will strengthen their understanding of international law while contributing to the promotion of legal cooperation and the rule of law worldwide. With fully funded fellowship opportunities available, this programme provides an exceptional platform for legal professionals seeking to expand their expertise and advance their careers within international legal and diplomatic spheres.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand (Fully Funded Fellowship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027: Fully Supported Global Convenings for Changemakers Tackling the World’s Biggest Challenges
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027, a globally recognized initiative that brings together visionary leaders, organizations, innovators, policymakers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers from around the world to develop breakthrough solutions to humanity’s most pressing challenges. For more than six decades, The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center has served […]

The post Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027: Fully Supported Global Convenings for Changemakers Tackling the World’s Biggest Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027, a globally recognized initiative that brings together visionary leaders, organizations, innovators, policymakers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers from around the world to develop breakthrough solutions to humanity’s most pressing challenges.

For more than six decades, The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center has served as one of the world’s leading spaces for collaboration, innovation, and global problem-solving. Located in Bellagio, Italy, the Center has hosted influential convenings that have contributed to transformative initiatives across health, food systems, energy access, finance, climate action, and international development.

The 2027 Bellagio Center Convening Program offers organizations a unique opportunity to host high-impact, one-week convenings designed to accelerate solutions, build strategic partnerships, foster collaboration, and create lasting global impact.

Each year, approximately 1,000 participants attend around 60 Bellagio convenings, making the program one of the most influential global platforms for advancing ideas that can improve lives worldwide.

About The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center

The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center is widely recognized as a global hub for collaboration, innovation, and social impact.

Its mission is to bring together leaders from different sectors, disciplines, countries, and perspectives to:

  • Develop innovative solutions.
  • Create transformative partnerships.
  • Advance social and economic progress.
  • Address global challenges.
  • Promote sustainable development.
  • Improve the well-being of vulnerable and underserved populations.

For over 60 years, the Bellagio Center has been a place where leaders come together to think boldly, collaborate meaningfully, and generate breakthrough ideas capable of changing systems at scale.

What Is the Bellagio Center Convening Program?

The Bellagio Center Convening Program supports organizations seeking to bring together carefully selected participants around a specific challenge, opportunity, or solution.

These convenings are designed to:

  • Strengthen promising solutions.
  • Build cross-sector partnerships.
  • Facilitate collaboration.
  • Generate actionable outcomes.
  • Advance global development goals.
  • Create lasting social impact.

The program recognizes that transformative breakthroughs rarely happen in isolation. Instead, they emerge when diverse stakeholders share expertise, challenge assumptions, and collaborate toward common goals.

Global Impact of Bellagio Convenings

The Bellagio Center has played a critical role in launching and advancing several globally influential initiatives, including:

Global Energy Alliance (GEA)

A major initiative focused on expanding access to clean and affordable energy globally.

Global Alliance for Vaccines and Immunization (GAVI)

One of the world’s most successful public health partnerships, improving access to vaccines worldwide.

CGIAR

A leading global agricultural research partnership focused on food security and sustainable agriculture.

Global Impact Investing Network (GIIN)

An influential initiative promoting investment strategies that generate both social and financial returns.

These examples demonstrate how Bellagio convenings can catalyze major global movements and solutions.

What the Program Offers

Selected organizations gain access to one of the world’s most respected convening spaces and benefit from:

1. World-Class Convening Environment

Organizations can host their convening at the historic Bellagio Center in Italy.

2. Global Networking Opportunities

Conveners connect with:

  • Global leaders.
  • Policymakers.
  • Philanthropists.
  • Researchers.
  • Civil society organizations.
  • Entrepreneurs.
  • Development practitioners.

3. Strategic Collaboration

Participants benefit from meaningful interdisciplinary engagement that encourages innovation and collective problem-solving.

4. Enhanced Visibility

Bellagio convenings often attract attention from influential stakeholders and institutions.

5. Opportunity to Accelerate Impact

Convenings help move ideas from discussion to implementation by creating actionable plans and partnerships.

What Makes a Successful Convening?

The Rockefeller Foundation identifies several characteristics of successful Bellagio convenings.

Advance a Specific Solution

Proposals should focus on advancing a clearly defined solution capable of generating meaningful breakthroughs.

Demonstrate Charitable Purpose

Convenings should contribute to improving the lives of vulnerable and underserved populations.

Show a Clear Path to Impact

Applicants must explain how the convening’s outcomes will continue creating impact after the event concludes.

Engage Key Stakeholders

Strong proposals demonstrate a thoughtful strategy for involving individuals and organizations capable of advancing the solution.

Leverage a Catalytic Moment

Applicants should show why the timing is important and how the convening can capitalize on current opportunities.

Demonstrate Organizational Capacity

Organizations must show they possess the resources, expertise, and commitment necessary to organize and follow through on the convening.

Priority Themes for 2027

The Rockefeller Foundation is particularly interested in convenings aligned with seven strategic themes.

1. Universal Energy Abundance

Focus areas include:

  • Clean energy access.
  • Renewable technologies.
  • Energy storage innovations.
  • Energy affordability.
  • Energy equity.
  • Climate resilience.

2. Food Systems and Nutrition

Priority topics include:

  • Sustainable agriculture.
  • Food security.
  • Nutrition.
  • Farmer resilience.
  • Climate-smart agriculture.
  • Food distribution systems.

3. Economic Mobility

Convenings may address:

  • Poverty reduction.
  • Workforce development.
  • Financial inclusion.
  • Skills training.
  • Economic opportunity.
  • Community investment.

4. Health

Potential topics include:

  • Climate-related health challenges.
  • Public health systems.
  • Community resilience.
  • Extreme heat impacts.
  • Health innovation.
  • Global health coordination.

5. Artificial Intelligence for Good

Applicants may explore:

  • Responsible AI.
  • AI governance.
  • Educational applications.
  • Public health applications.
  • Ethical AI deployment.
  • Equity and inclusion in AI systems.

6. Catalytic Finance

Relevant areas include:

  • Blended finance.
  • Impact investing.
  • Development finance.
  • Climate finance.
  • Innovative funding mechanisms.
  • Financial inclusion.

7. Climate and Development

Topics may include:

  • Climate adaptation.
  • Sustainable development.
  • Infrastructure resilience.
  • Environmental protection.
  • Community adaptation.
  • Climate-responsive policy.

Who Should Apply?

The Bellagio Center Convening Program welcomes applications from organizations and leaders capable of convening diverse stakeholders around important global challenges.

Potential applicants include:

  • Nonprofit organizations.
  • Civil society organizations.
  • Universities.
  • Research institutions.
  • Think tanks.
  • Foundations.
  • Social enterprises.
  • International organizations.
  • Policy networks.
  • Multi-sector coalitions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Bellagio Center Convening Program is not simply an event-hosting opportunity. It is a platform designed to generate tangible progress on issues that affect millions of people worldwide.

Successful convenings can:

  • Influence policy.
  • Launch partnerships.
  • Mobilize funding.
  • Create new networks.
  • Advance research.
  • Accelerate innovation.
  • Drive systemic change.

Few global platforms offer the same level of prestige, influence, and potential for long-term impact.

Location

Bellagio Center

Bellagio, Italy

The Bellagio Center provides a unique environment designed to encourage focused collaboration, strategic thinking, and creative problem-solving.

Application Timeline

The Rockefeller Foundation is currently accepting proposals for the 2027 Bellagio Center Convening Program till June 23, 2026.

Applicants should consult the Official Application Materials and FAQs page for detailed review timelines, eligibility requirements, travel support information, and selection procedures.

How to Apply

Interested organizations must complete and submit the Official Bellagio Center Convening Program Application.

Applications should clearly explain:

  • The challenge being addressed.
  • The proposed solution.
  • Expected outcomes.
  • Stakeholder engagement strategy.
  • Long-term impact plan.
  • Organizational capacity.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027.

Conclusion

The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027 represents a remarkable opportunity for organizations seeking to accelerate solutions to some of the world’s most urgent challenges. By bringing together diverse leaders, experts, practitioners, and changemakers in one of the world’s most respected convening spaces, the program creates the conditions necessary for breakthrough ideas to emerge and thrive. Whether focused on climate resilience, food security, economic mobility, health innovation, artificial intelligence, energy access, or catalytic finance, successful convenings have the potential to influence systems, strengthen partnerships, and improve lives worldwide. Organizations with bold ideas and a commitment to meaningful impact should strongly consider applying for this globally prestigious opportunity.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Convening Program 2027: Fully Supported Global Convenings for Changemakers Tackling the World’s Biggest Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) at BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC), Canada: Earn Up to CAD $110,800 Annually While Advancing Human Rights Research
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Are you passionate about research, social justice, human rights, equity, and creating meaningful change through evidence-based work? The BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC) in Canada is currently recruiting a Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) for a full-time temporary position. This is an exceptional opportunity for professionals with strong research expertise who want […]

The post Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) at BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC), Canada: Earn Up to CAD $110,800 Annually While Advancing Human Rights Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about research, social justice, human rights, equity, and creating meaningful change through evidence-based work? The BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC) in Canada is currently recruiting a Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) for a full-time temporary position. This is an exceptional opportunity for professionals with strong research expertise who want to contribute to addressing inequality, discrimination, and systemic injustice while working in a dynamic and impactful environment.

The position offers a competitive salary package, the flexibility to work from anywhere in British Columbia, and the chance to contribute to influential public research that helps shape conversations, policies, and practices related to human rights.

Applications are currently open and interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

About BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC)

The BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner exists to tackle the root causes of discrimination, inequality, and injustice across British Columbia. Rather than focusing solely on individual complaints, the organization works to create systemic change through:

  • Research
  • Public education
  • Advocacy
  • Monitoring
  • Inquiries
  • Policy influence

The office aims to transform laws, institutions, practices, and cultural systems that contribute to inequity and discrimination.

Through evidence-based research and collaboration with communities, BCOHRC seeks to amplify marginalized voices and ensure that public decision-making reflects the experiences of people affected by human rights challenges.

Position Overview

Job Title

Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2)

Competition Number

123889

Employment Type

  • Temporary
  • Full-time
  • 12-month contract
  • Possibility of extension

Location

Anywhere in British Columbia, Canada

Salary

The successful candidate will receive an attractive compensation package:

  • Salary Range: CAD $78,400.17 – $110,800.21 annually
  • Starting Salary: CAD $89,140.15 annually

This salary package reflects the importance of the role and the expertise required to conduct high-level research on human rights issues.

Role and Responsibilities

Reporting directly to the Manager, Research, the Research Officer will play a critical role in designing, implementing, and supporting strategic research initiatives related to human rights issues in British Columbia.

The successful candidate will be expected to:

Conduct Research

  • Design and carry out primary research projects
  • Conduct secondary research and literature reviews
  • Utilize both qualitative and quantitative research methodologies
  • Analyze complex datasets and evidence

Produce High-Quality Reports

The Research Officer will prepare:

  • Public research reports
  • Internal research documents
  • Briefing notes
  • Evidence summaries
  • Knowledge products

These publications help:

  • Highlight systemic human rights issues
  • Amplify the voices of people with lived experience
  • Inform public discourse
  • Support policy development

Collaborate Across Teams

The role involves working closely with:

  • Interdisciplinary research teams
  • Community organizations
  • Human rights advocates
  • Stakeholders
  • People with lived experience

Support Decolonial Research Approaches

BCOHRC emphasizes research grounded in the 4Rs framework:

  1. Responsibility
  2. Reciprocity
  3. Relevance
  4. Respect

Researchers are expected to ensure that their work reflects these principles and contributes to ethical, community-centered knowledge production.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Unlike many traditional research roles, this position directly contributes to real-world human rights improvements.

Key benefits include:

1. Meaningful Impact

Your research can influence:

  • Public policy
  • Human rights conversations
  • Government decisions
  • Organizational practices

2. Competitive Compensation

Earn a starting salary of over CAD $89,000 annually with opportunities within a salary range exceeding CAD $110,000.

3. Flexible Work Location

The position can be performed from anywhere within British Columbia.

4. Professional Growth

Gain experience in:

  • Human rights research
  • Public policy analysis
  • Community engagement
  • Mixed-methods research
  • Strategic advocacy

5. Collaborative Environment

Work alongside professionals dedicated to advancing equity, justice, and systemic change.

Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Commitment

BCOHRC is actively working to build a diverse workforce with lived experiences relevant to human rights issues.

Preference may be given to applicants who identify as:

  • Indigenous peoples
  • Black individuals
  • People of Colour
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Individuals with diverse gender identities or expressions
  • Individuals from diverse sexual orientations

The organization recognizes the impact of discrimination and approaches recruitment using trauma-informed principles.

Additionally, First Nations, Inuit, and Métis applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as part of broader efforts to address the impacts of colonization in Canada.

Conflict of Interest Considerations

As an independent officer of the Legislature, BCOHRC places significant importance on maintaining independence and public trust.

Successful candidates may be required to step down from:

  • Governance positions
  • Board memberships
  • Leadership roles
  • Activities that could create actual, potential, or perceived conflicts of interest

This ensures the credibility and impartiality of the organization’s work.

Important Employment Conditions

Applicants should note the following requirements:

Eligibility to Work in Canada

Candidates must be:

  • Canadian citizens, or
  • Permanent residents of Canada, or
  • Individuals legally authorized to work in Canada through a valid work permit

Employment remains contingent upon maintaining legal authorization to work in Canada.

Criminal Record Check

The selected candidate will undergo a criminal record check.

Importantly, having a criminal record does not automatically disqualify an applicant.

Dynamic Work Environment

The position involves:

  • Managing multiple priorities
  • Responding to emerging organizational needs
  • Meeting competing deadlines
  • Working effectively under changing circumstances

Strong organizational and time-management skills are therefore essential.

Application Process

Interested candidates must submit:

Required Documents

  1. Cover Letter
  2. Resume/CV

Applicants should clearly demonstrate:

  • Relevant educational background
  • Research experience
  • Human rights-related experience
  • Qualifications that align with the job profile

Application Instructions

  • Include the position title in the cover letter.
  • Include the position title in the email subject line.
  • Submit applications directly by email.

Applications should be sent to:

recruitment@bchumanrights.ca

Only applications submitted directly to BCOHRC via email will be considered.

Applications submitted through the BC Government Hiring Centre website will not be accepted.

Selection Process

Shortlisted candidates may be invited to participate in:

Stage 1

  • Screening interview and/or written assignment

Stage 2

  • Formal interview

The quality and clarity of the cover letter and resume may form part of the assessment process.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to provide detailed examples demonstrating how they meet each qualification requirement.

Deadline

Application Deadline: June 15, 2026, at 5:00 PM Pacific Time (PT)

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) position at BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner represents a remarkable opportunity for researchers who want their work to contribute to meaningful social change. With an attractive salary, flexible work arrangements, opportunities for collaboration, and the ability to influence human rights policy and public discourse, this role is ideal for professionals committed to advancing equity and justice.

Candidates with strong research skills, experience in qualitative and quantitative methodologies, and a passion for human rights are encouraged to apply before the June 15, 2026 deadline.

 

How to apply

Review Job Profile(opens in a new window)
  1. Submit a cover letter and resume detailing your relevant work experience and how you meet the stated qualifications.
  2. Include the position title on the cover letter and in the email subject line.
  3. Applications must be submitted by email to recruitment@bchumanrights.ca

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here

More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Research Officer, Generalist (Band 2) at BC’s Office of the Human Rights Commissioner (BCOHRC), Canada: Earn Up to CAD $110,800 Annually While Advancing Human Rights Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme 2027: Fully Supported Fellowship at ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute in Singapore
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme 2027 is now open for applications for post-doctoral researchers and mid-career policy makers interested in producing policy-relevant research on Southeast Asia, big-power relations, regional security, economics, and digital technology. Hosted by ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute in Singapore, the programme honours Professor Wang Gungwu, former Chairman of the Board […]

The post Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme 2027: Fully Supported Fellowship at ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute in Singapore appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme 2027 is now open for applications for post-doctoral researchers and mid-career policy makers interested in producing policy-relevant research on Southeast Asia, big-power relations, regional security, economics, and digital technology.

Hosted by ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute in Singapore, the programme honours Professor Wang Gungwu, former Chairman of the Board of Trustees at ISEAS, in recognition of his distinguished 17-year tenure and his major contributions to scholarship, public policy, and the study of Asia.

This visiting fellowship is designed to support the next generation of scholars and policy thinkers whose work examines how international political, economic, security, and technological developments shape Southeast Asia. Successful applicants will be based at ISEAS for the duration of the fellowship and will contribute to the Institute’s research community through publications, seminars, and policy discussions.

About the Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme

The Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme supports researchers and policy makers working on issues connected to big-power relations and their impact on Southeast Asia.

The programme is particularly focused on research that is not only academically strong but also relevant to public policy, regional decision-making, and wider debates on Southeast Asia’s role in a changing global order.

Through this fellowship, ISEAS aims to create a platform where emerging and established voices can engage deeply with regional developments, contribute fresh perspectives, and build networks with scholars, policy experts, and practitioners working on Southeast Asian affairs.

Host Institution: ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute

The fellowship is hosted by ISEAS – Yusof Ishak Institute, a leading research institute based in Singapore. ISEAS is widely known for its work on Southeast Asian studies, regional politics, economics, society, culture, and international relations.

Fellows will be based at the Institute during their fellowship period and will have access to research facilities, office space, library resources, IT services, and opportunities to engage with the wider ISEAS academic and policy community.

Fellowship Duration

The Wang Gungwu Visiting Fellows Programme may run for:

  1. A minimum period of two months
  2. A maximum period of six months

The exact duration of the fellowship is subject to approval by ISEAS.

Successful applicants are expected to be in residence at ISEAS in Singapore for the full duration of their fellowship. This means selected fellows should be prepared to physically relocate to Singapore for the approved fellowship period.

Research Priority Areas

Applicants should propose research that fits within the programme’s key priority areas. The fellowship focuses on the relationship between major global powers and Southeast Asia, with special attention to politics, security, economics, technology, society, and regional influence.

The priority areas include:

1. Developments in US-China Relations and Their Impact on Southeast Asia

Applicants may explore how changing relations between the United States and China affect Southeast Asian countries, regional institutions, foreign policy choices, trade, security alignments, and strategic decision-making.

Possible research themes may include:

  • The effect of US-China competition on ASEAN unity
  • Southeast Asian foreign policy strategies in a period of big-power rivalry
  • Economic consequences of US-China tensions for Southeast Asian economies
  • Security partnerships and diplomatic balancing in the region
  • Regional responses to geopolitical competition

2. Asia Pacific Security Issues

The programme welcomes research on major security questions affecting the Asia Pacific and Southeast Asia.

Examples of relevant topics include:

  • South China Sea disputes
  • China-ASEAN relations
  • Maritime security
  • Regional defence cooperation
  • ASEAN’s role in regional security architecture
  • Military modernisation and strategic competition
  • Security implications of big-power rivalry

This priority area is suitable for applicants interested in how Southeast Asian countries respond to complex and evolving security challenges.

3. Global and Regional Economic Cooperation Initiatives

Applicants may also focus on regional and global economic initiatives and their implications for Southeast Asia.

Relevant topics may include:

  • Belt and Road Initiative
  • Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership
  • Trade integration in Southeast Asia
  • Regional economic cooperation
  • Infrastructure financing
  • Supply chain shifts
  • Economic diplomacy
  • The role of Southeast Asia in global trade networks

This area is especially relevant for researchers examining how large-scale economic initiatives shape national development, regional cooperation, and geopolitical influence.

4. The Rise of China and Its Impact on Southeast Asian Communities

The programme also invites research on the social, cultural, political, and economic effects of China’s rise on communities across Southeast Asia.

Possible research directions include:

  • Chinese investment and local communities
  • Perceptions of China in Southeast Asian societies
  • Chinese diaspora and community relations
  • Social and cultural influence
  • Domestic political responses to China’s rise
